summary:
Dubin-Johnson syndrome uniquely does not increase unconjugated bilirubin because it involves defects in the export of *conjugated* bilirubin from liver cells, affecting only the post-conjugative phase. All other listed disorders—Crigler-Najjar, Gilbert, and hemolytic anemia—increase unconjugated bilirubin either by reducing conjugation capacity or overwhelming hepatic processing.  

\boxed{B}

All other listed disorders—Crigler-Najjar, Gilbert, and hemolytic anemia—increase unconjugated bilirubin either by reducing conjugation capacity or overwhelming hepatic processing.  

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
